analysis:
1. NHL Stanley Cup — Colorado Avalanche Surge

The Colorado Avalanche are the most actionable live market in this dataset. At 23.3% to win the 2026 Stanley Cup, they gained +1.25pp this week on $11.7M volume — the largest absolute weekly gain among high-probability markets. The day change of -1.25pp suggests some near-term pullback after the weekly run-up, indicating active two-way trading rather than a one-directional drift. No other NHL team appears in the top 50 by volume, making Colorado the de facto market consensus favorite by default.

2. Champions League — Fringe Contenders Getting Priced In

Bodø/Glimt is the standout mover in Champions League markets, jumping +1pp this week to 2.7% — a 59% relative increase — on $14.6M volume, the highest of any UCL market in the dataset. Galatasaray also gained +0.6pp to 1.0% and Atalanta +0.7pp to 0.1%, signaling that market participants are rotating into long-shot UCL positions, likely tied to recent match results in the knockout rounds. Club Brugge (-0.3pp), Slavia Prague (-0.1pp), Athletic Club (-0.2pp), and Benfica (-0.2pp) all fell this week, reinforcing a reallocation away from eliminated or underperforming sides toward the survivors.

3. NBA — Luka Doncic MVP Collapse, Finals Field Wide Open

Luka Dončić's NBA MVP odds have collapsed to just 1.4%, down -1.3pp this week and -9.6pp over the past month — a near-total wipeout, likely reflecting his trade to the Lakers and/or injury/performance concerns. The NBA Finals market is highly fragmented: Charlotte Hornets lead this dataset's listed contenders at just 1.0% (down -0.35pp this week), with the LA Clippers at 2.3% (down -0.15pp) and Golden State Warriors at 1.2% in the Western Conference Finals market (down -0.25pp). No single team commands meaningful probability, pointing to genuine uncertainty or the absence of the top favorites from this particular data slice.

4. Premier League & Ligue 1 — Settled Races, Fading Long Shots

The 2025–26 Premier League market shows near-universal 0.1% pricing across Chelsea, Leeds, Tottenham, Aston Villa, Manchester United, and 10+ other clubs — the title race is effectively over in market terms, with Liverpool implicitly the consensus winner given all competitors are priced at noise levels. Aston Villa is the week's notable decliner at -0.65pp (now 0.2%), extending a brutal -5.2pp monthly drop. In Ligue 1, Lens fell -0.75pp this week to 11.4%, though they remain the second most likely champion in that market — their month-over-month gain of +3.15pp suggests recent form had boosted them before this week's reversal.

5. Key Shifts This Week

**Biggest mover:** Luka Dončić MVP down -1.3pp to 1.4%, capping a -9.6pp monthly freefall — effectively a dead market. **Colorado Avalanche** gained +1.25pp to 23.3%, the largest upward move in absolute terms among liquid markets. **Bodø/Glimt** added +1.0pp to 2.7% in UCL, the strongest relative gain in that competition's markets. **Aston Villa** EPL shed -0.65pp to 0.2%, continuing a month-long collapse from what was once a non-trivial title contention price. **Lens** in Ligue 1 dropped -0.75pp to 11.4% after a strong monthly run, suggesting a specific negative catalyst this week.
